jokes.text: Where's the remote? This is not a drill, people.
jokes.setup: Someone looking for a TV remote
jokes.punchline: Treating it as an emergency requiring military-level urgency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with tight economy - transforms mundane situation into military emergency

jokes.text: Hmm, the TV changes channels every time the dog barks. We return to Mel Gibson's The Salad of the Christ on Christian Carrot Theater.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous religious parody show title combining 'Passion of the Christ' with vegetables
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdist humor with strong wordplay - the vegetable religious parody is memorably ridiculous

jokes.text: I think he swallowed the remote. Barf for your master.
jokes.setup: Trying to get the dog to vomit up the remote
jokes.punchline: The degrading command 'barf for your master'
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy setup with Homer's typical lack of dignity

jokes.text: Lousy American-made dog.
jokes.punchline: Blaming the dog's nationality rather than training
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - blaming nationality for personal problems, delivered with great economy

jokes.text: Why must I be so voluptuous?
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating comment about her attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's unexpected vanity contrasts with her usual modesty

jokes.text: Yo, yo, yo. Here now the nizzews. The top artists in hip-hop are coming to Springfield.
jokes.punchline: News anchor awkwardly attempting hip-hop slang with 'nizzews' for news
jokes.explanation: Good satire of media trying to be hip - the forced slang transformation is well-executed

jokes.text: Da Glock Pointaz, Romeo Smoov, Queen Booty Shaykah, M.C. Champagne Millionaire, and Assault Weapons magazine's Man of the Year, Alcatraaaz
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and violent rapper names, especially 'Assault Weapons magazine's Man of the Year'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd rapper names with dark humor twist - the award reference is particularly clever

jokes.text: Just what we need-- another lame suburban kid who loves rap. So? You like the blues. Yes, but the blues are unpopular.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pretentious justification that blues are superior because they're unpopular
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - her intellectual snobbery expressed through musical preference hierarchy

jokes.text: Rappers stopped saying 'illin'' 12 years ago. I'm keepin' it real. They stopped saying 'keepin' it real' three years ago.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's precise timeline of when rap slang became outdated
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating pattern with Lisa's encyclopedic knowledge of rap terminology expiration dates - very funny concept

jokes.text: Dad, can I go to a rap concert? Tickets are $50. Go to hell. Okay, what if I pay for it myself? Fine, go nuts.
jokes.setup: Bart asking permission for expensive concert
jokes.punchline: Homer's instant attitude change when money isn't his problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - completely changes position based solely on financial self-interest

jokes.text: I love you, Dad. I love you too, Jerry.
jokes.punchline: Homer calling Bart by wrong name in touching moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - perfect timing and economy in ruining a sweet moment

jokes.text: It encourages punching, boastfulness, and rudeness to hos.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining problems with rap music
jokes.punchline: Marge saying 'hos' in her prim voice while listing objections
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Marge's wholesome delivery of hip-hop terminology creates great contrast

jokes.text: Step off, Mom. Rap is the poetry of the streets. Well, you are not going to any concert that promulgates stink talk.
jokes.setup: Bart defending rap as street poetry
jokes.punchline: Marge's prim word 'promulgates' combined with crude 'stink talk'
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Marge's formal vocabulary clashing with street slang criticism

jokes.text: promulgates stink talk
jokes.punchline: Formal word 'promulgates' with silly 'stink talk'
jokes.explanation: Marge's vocabulary mixing formal and childish terms

jokes.text: You did it on the straight Got your dad's permission But your mom dropped a bomb So I flipped my position Don't argue with Marge, I know what's best The only rap in this crib keeps sandwiches fresh
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his position change in rap style
jokes.punchline: Terrible rap performance ending with plastic wrap pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended joke - Homer's terrible rapping with dad-joke wordplay is perfectly in character and memorable

jokes.text: Dad, you completely sold me out.
jokes.punchline: Bart's deadpan reaction to the cringe
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that caps off Homer's rap sequence

jokes.text: Well, I paid for this ticket. That makes me an adult. I'm going.
jokes.punchline: Child logic that paying for something equals adulthood
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - the financial responsibility = adulthood equation is believable for Bart

jokes.text: Every miner '49er and stone-cold conniver Stakes a claim on my tongue to pan my saliva Make the hos drop they clothes like they Lady Godiva
jokes.setup: Bart attempting to rap on stage
jokes.punchline: 10-year-old delivering inappropriate lyrics with gold rush metaphors
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - child delivering adult content with historical references

jokes.text: You can trace my remorse to its supersized source A hungry, hungry hypocrite named Homer, of course My old man's pathetic Damn, is his head thick The gas from his ass is carcinogenic Every day I pray his DNA ain't genetic
jokes.punchline: Brutal roasting of Homer with clever wordplay including 'supersized' and DNA/genetic pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent rap sequence with great wordplay and perfect Bart characterization - the DNA/genetic line is particularly clever

jokes.text: Yo, B, I heard you throw down on stage. Wanna join my world tour? Sorry, Fiddy. I have school tomorrow. You're right-- the more you know, the further you'll go. And that's one to grow on.
jokes.setup: Famous rapper offering Bart a tour opportunity
jokes.punchline: 50 Cent delivering after-school special wisdom
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - hardcore rapper giving wholesome educational messages

jokes.text: Here you go, you little yellow cracker. Now let's go murder our enemies. Peace.
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting with violent language followed by 'Peace'
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction joke with perfect economy - wholesome to violent to peaceful in rapid succession

jokes.text: If that's true, he's gonna be like NWA-- Not Without Ass-welts.
jokes.punchline: Dad-joke pun on famous rap group NWA
jokes.explanation: Solid dad-joke pun that's perfectly in character for Homer's blend of pop culture awareness and corniness

jokes.text: Well, time to face my punishment like a man. Or... lie my way out of it like a kid.
jokes.punchline: Immediately choosing the dishonest but age-appropriate option
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart characterization - moral consideration immediately overruled by self-preservation instinct

jokes.text: 'Follow instructions'? He's doomed.
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate assessment of Homer's inability to follow directions
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Marge's instant recognition of Homer's incompetence in crisis

jokes.text: This'll be the worst Thanksgiving dinner ever. Thanksgiving's not for months. The bad news keeps on coming.
jokes.punchline: Learning it's nowhere near Thanksgiving makes it even worse somehow
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Homer's confusion about timing somehow makes the situation worse in his mind

jokes.text: Tragedy climbed a ladder last night As 10 year old Bart Simpson was snatch from his bed. His chocolate-milk dreams cut short by kidnapping themes.
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic language including 'chocolate-milk dreams'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of overblown news reporting with memorable poetic language

jokes.text: Mrs Simpson, is the pain so unbearable you wish your son had never been born?
jokes.punchline: Incredibly inappropriate and cruel question
jokes.explanation: Good media satire - reporter's complete lack of sensitivity

jokes.text: It's: 'win a hawaïan getaway week on channel 6.' So, mail in those pineapple stickers today. Aloha means 'one entry per person.'
jokes.setup: Reporter claiming to have comforting message for grieving mother
jokes.punchline: It's actually just a random contest promotion
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke satirizing news media priorities and insensitivity

jokes.text: Chief, seriously. Shouldn't we just plan for the funeral? Hey, I crack cases all the time. Like the case of the symphony conductor who murdered his star cellist. That was an episode of Columbo, Chief.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum claiming to solve cases
jokes.punchline: His 'solved case' was actually a TV show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum characterization - his incompetence revealed through confusion of reality and TV

jokes.text: They show you who the bad guy is at the beginning of each one. Yeah, but you have to remember.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum thinking remembering information is the challenging part
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Wiggum's incompetence - even the easiest mystery format is challenging for him

jokes.text: Bart. Are the kidnappers after you? Well, some kidnappers might be after me. It's a big world. But in this case, I faked the whole thing.
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual admission that he faked his own kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reveal with Bart's characteristic casual attitude toward major deception

jokes.text: Well, some kidnappers might be after me. It's a big world.
jokes.setup: Milhouse asking if kidnappers are after Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual acknowledgment of theoretical kidnapping danger
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of absurd worldview

jokes.text: In my dad's apartment, the heat is always off. He made a coat out of all my stuffed animals.
jokes.setup: Offering Bart a place to hide
jokes.punchline: Revealing his dad's pathetic living situation through stuffed animal coat
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - the stuffed animal coat is a perfectly absurd detail that reveals Kirk's desperation

jokes.text: Shoo. Get out of here. These aren't your soybeans.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic job defending soybeans from unknown threat
jokes.explanation: Adds to Kirk's pathetic character - meaningless job with meaningless authority

jokes.text: Wigum sleeps through riot, Top cop surrenders to backfiring car, Firemen rescue police chief from tree, Commission: Wiggum sucks
jokes.setup: Wiggum reading newspaper headlines about himself
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of incompetent incidents culminating in blunt headline
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building to the blunt 'Wiggum sucks' - perfect character assassination through headlines

jokes.text: Together, they really paint a picture... of failure.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's moment of self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Wiggum self-reflection

jokes.text: My fat grew over my badge.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's weight problem literally covering his badge
jokes.explanation: Good character detail with excellent economy - perfectly captures Wiggum's physical state

jokes.text: Set? Are you the character or the actor who plays him? Now I must go... Wait a minute, now you're a ghost? Avenge me.
jokes.punchline: Confusion about reality levels, sudden ghost transformation
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on fictional characters, gets confusing

jokes.text: You can use any section of the paper. Except Metro. It's a big snooze.
jokes.punchline: Homer being picky about newspaper sections during fake ransom call
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - even during crisis, he has opinions about newspaper quality

jokes.text: We have a lab? Yeah, it's that room we keep the Christmas decorations in.
jokes.punchline: Springfield police 'lab' is storage closet
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of police department's inadequate resources

jokes.text: Hail falling on a tin roof? No. That's it! Stovetop popcorn. And it sounds like my favorite brand Chintzy-Pop!
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to identify background noise
jokes.punchline: His 'detective work' is just recognizing cheap popcorn he personally uses
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Wiggum's 'brilliant' deduction is just personal familiarity with junk food

jokes.text: Oh, Chintzy-Pop is the worst legal popcorn. Many of the kernels are baby teeth.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing revelation about baby teeth in popcorn
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark absurdist humor - the baby teeth detail is memorably disturbing and funny

jokes.text: Drop the corn, tightey-whitey!
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's attempt at tough cop talk with underwear insult
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Wiggum's juvenile approach to law enforcement

jokes.text: There's no need for you to defend your captor, Bratty Hearst.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's mangled reference to Patty Hearst syndrome
jokes.explanation: Good character joke - Wiggum's attempt at psychological insight with name pun

jokes.text: Taking your tombstone back to the store will be the happiest thing I've ever done.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing relief at Bart's safety
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of having already bought a tombstone
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - Marge's practical approach to grief preparation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You're 'Worried In West Springfield?' Not any more, Chief. Not any more.
jokes.punchline: Lou reveals he was anonymous critic, now converted
jokes.explanation: Nice callback revealing Lou's dual loyalty

jokes.text: Bart, two, three, four! You put my Dad in jail, flip, spin, twirl!
jokes.punchline: Adding dance moves to his angry accusation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse characterization - his weak attempt at confrontation includes choreography

jokes.text: I wasn't scared, I was just peeing.
jokes.setup: Bart threatening to reveal embarrassing secret
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's pathetic attempt to save face
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse logic - somehow thinks his explanation makes it better

jokes.text: Boys, even though I've been made Police Commissioner, don't think I've forgotten you. Lou, you're promoted to Chief of Police. Sweet. Eddie, you're promoted to Lou. Nice. Who's gonna be Eddie? We don't need an Eddie.
jokes.punchline: Circular promotion system that eliminates Eddie's role entirely
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - the promotion chain that ends with role elimination is perfectly illogical

jokes.text: That kid can't understand where the world goes when you close the drapes.
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific example of child-like confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - perfectly captures both Ralph's innocence and Wiggum's paternal understanding

jokes.text: Kirk, you are so dangerous and misunderstood! You've kidnapped my heart! I love the way I've heard of you!
jokes.setup: Women supposedly attracted to dangerous felons
jokes.punchline: Shallow attraction based merely on having heard of someone
jokes.explanation: Good satire of celebrity culture and criminal fascination - the 'heard of you' line is particularly shallow

jokes.text: What shirt? I don't see any shirt.
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate denial of obvious evidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer denial of reality

jokes.text: Burn, truth, burn. Burn, truth, burn.
jokes.punchline: Chanting like he's destroying an enemy rather than just truth
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's casual relationship with truth and morality

jokes.text: Our school's second most-wanted criminal after the mysterious El Barto?
jokes.punchline: Skinner doesn't know Bart IS El Barto
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Skinner hunting Bart while unaware he's already caught his other target

jokes.text: She'll hunt us down relentlessly like a bloodhound mixed with a student loan officer.
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to the most relentless pursuer imaginable - debt collectors
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor combining animal instinct with bureaucratic persistence - perfectly captures Lisa's nature

jokes.text: I just printed up 10,000 business cards. How am I going to pass all these out before she catches me?
jokes.punchline: Main concern is distributing business cards for fake promotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum priorities - business cards more important than avoiding jail

jokes.text: Wait, before we go, I took the liberty of making these embroidered conspiracy jackets for all of us. Those jackets are beautiful. We must never wear them.
jokes.punchline: Recognizing they're too incriminating to actually wear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - creating evidence of conspiracy then immediately realizing the problem

jokes.text: I've got a 200-inch plasma TV. In my shoe closet.
jokes.punchline: Massive TV in inappropriate location
jokes.explanation: Standard rapper wealth absurdity

jokes.text: This is hopeless. My gun isn't even loaded.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of police incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Wiggum's incompetence extends to basic police equipment

jokes.text: I guess I'll go back to Baltimore and sell ribbon with my Daddy. Ribbon! Get your ribbon! Great for presents!
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific family ribbon business with sad sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist detail - the family ribbon business is perfectly random and pathetic

jokes.text: Coolee, coolee cool! C'mon, fool! Be coolee, coolee cool!
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Lisa to be cool
jokes.punchline: Middle-aged authority figures chanting made-up cool slang
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing adults trying desperately to be hip

jokes.text: Do you think there's a place in the hip-hop world for a... 40-something elementary school administrator? Hell yes. But I'm already payin' a guy for that.
jokes.punchline: Rapper's encouraging response followed by immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with perfect timing - builds up hope then crushes it immediately

jokes.text: I think they're making fun of me, but my wife is very sick.
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about wife's illness as explanation for staying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - his social awkwardness and tragic personal life collide

jokes.text: Do you think there's a place in the hip-hop world for a... 40-something elementary school administrator? Hell yes. But I'm already payin' a guy for that.
jokes.punchline: Position exists but is already filled
jokes.explanation: Good absurd premise - hip-hop needs school administrators
